title: Knowledge and Wisdom
introduction: Chapter 7 of the Bhagavad Gita, Jnana Vijnana Yoga (The Yoga of Knowledge and Realization), is one of the most profound and reassuring chapters in the entire text. Krishna now moves from practical instructions on action, meditation, and discipline into direct revelation of who He really is — and what the ultimate reality looks like. This is the chapter where Krishna says, in essence: 'Let me tell you the truth about Myself, about this universe, about why people suffer, and about how the rare few come to truly know Me.' For modern readers — especially those who feel spiritually curious yet overwhelmed by theories, paths, and self-improvement noise — Chapter 7 is pure clarity. Krishna distinguishes between lower knowledge (material world, science, psychology) and higher knowledge (direct realization of the Divine). He explains why most people miss the truth, why some rare souls find it, and how devotion (bhakti) becomes the simplest and most direct bridge to the Supreme. In 2026 life — full of information overload, existential anxiety, endless scrolling, and spiritual shopping — this chapter is the reset button: stop chasing concepts, start knowing the Person/Source behind everything. The essence remains untouched: true wisdom is not accumulating more ideas, but recognizing the Divine as the only real substance of existence, and approaching that Divine with love and surrender.

shlokas.sanskrit:
श्रीभगवानुवाच |
मय्यासक्तमनाः पार्थ योगं युञ्जन्मदाश्रयः |
असंशयं समग्रं मां यथा ज्ञास्यसि तच्छृणु ||
shlokas.transliteration:
śrī-bhagavān uvāca
mayy āsakta-manāḥ pārtha yogaṁ yuñjan mad-āśrayaḥ
asaṁśayaṁ samagraṁ māṁ yathā jñāsyasi tac chṛṇu
shlokas.translation: The Supreme Personality of Godhead said: Now hear, O son of Pṛthā, how by practicing yoga in full consciousness of Me, with mind attached to Me, you can know Me completely without doubt.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna invites Arjuna to listen with full attention so he can know the Supreme completely without any doubt by practicing yoga while keeping the mind attached to the divine. This verse opens the chapter on knowledge and wisdom by offering a direct method to understand the highest reality. For people in their thirties who seek clarity in career and life decisions this verse brings practical hope. For example a manager who begins each day by consciously offering his work to the divine and keeps his focus steady discovers deeper insight into his role and purpose. Detailed meaning: Krishna emphasizes that yoga practiced with exclusive attachment to Him leads to complete knowledge free from doubt. This is the foundation of transcendental wisdom where personal effort meets divine grace. The verse makes readers eager to apply this focused practice. Practice: start your day by fixing your mind on the divine and perform your duties as an offering so you gain clear understanding without confusion.
shlokas.genz: Krishna invites Arjuna to listen carefully so he can know the Supreme completely without any doubt by practicing yoga while keeping the mind attached to the divine. This verse opens the chapter on knowledge and wisdom by offering a direct method to understand the highest reality. For young adults who want real insight amid distractions this verse brings practical hope. For example a content creator who begins each project by consciously connecting to higher energy and keeps focus steady discovers deeper meaning in his work and life. Detailed meaning: Krishna emphasizes that yoga practiced with exclusive attachment to Him leads to complete knowledge free from doubt. This is the foundation of transcendental wisdom where personal effort meets divine grace. The verse makes you eager to apply this focused practice. Practice: start your day by fixing your mind on the divine and perform your moves as an offering so you gain clear understanding without confusion.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna invites Arjuna to listen carefully so he can know the Supreme completely without any doubt by practicing yoga while keeping the mind attached to the divine. This verse opens the chapter on knowledge and wisdom by offering a direct method to understand the highest reality. For children this is like a big friend asking you to listen closely so you can know the big secret without confusion by playing with a happy heart attached to the big friend. For example a child who starts each game by thinking of the big friend and keeps focus steady discovers deeper fun in every play. Detailed meaning: Krishna emphasizes that yoga practiced with exclusive attachment to Him leads to complete knowledge free from doubt. This is the foundation of transcendental wisdom where personal effort meets divine grace. The verse makes you curious to apply this focused practice. Practice: start your day by fixing your mind on the divine and perform your games as an offering so you gain clear understanding without confusion.

shlokas.sanskrit:
ज्ञानं तेऽहं सविज्ञानमिदं वक्ष्याम्यशेषतः |
यज्ज्ञात्वा नेह भूयोऽन्यज्ज्ञातव्यमवशिष्यते ||
shlokas.transliteration:
jñānaṁ te ’haṁ sa-vijñānam idaṁ vakṣyāmy aśeṣataḥ
yaj jñātvā neha bhūyo ’nyaj jñātavyam avaśiṣyate
shlokas.translation: I shall now declare unto you in full this knowledge both phenomenal and noumenal, by knowing which there shall remain nothing further to be known.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna promises to reveal complete knowledge both material and spiritual so that nothing more remains to be known. This verse presents the comprehensive nature of divine wisdom. For people in their thirties who seek holistic understanding in career and life this verse offers the ultimate knowledge base. For example a strategist who learns both practical skills and spiritual principles finds every decision becomes clear and complete. Detailed meaning: Krishna assures that this knowledge encompasses everything leaving no gap. This is the beauty of transcendental wisdom where all questions are answered. The verse makes readers eager to receive this full revelation. Practice: open your heart to receive both worldly and spiritual knowledge so your understanding becomes whole and nothing remains unknown.
shlokas.genz: Krishna promises to reveal complete knowledge both material and spiritual so that nothing more remains to be known. This verse presents the comprehensive nature of divine wisdom. For young adults who want the full picture amid information overload this verse offers the ultimate knowledge base. For example a learner who absorbs both practical tips and deeper truths finds every question answered and life makes sense. Detailed meaning: Krishna assures that this knowledge encompasses everything leaving no gap. This is the beauty of transcendental wisdom where all questions are answered. The verse makes you eager to receive this full revelation. Practice: open your heart to receive both worldly and spiritual knowledge so your understanding becomes whole and nothing remains unknown.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna promises to reveal complete knowledge both material and spiritual so that nothing more remains to be known. This verse presents the comprehensive nature of divine wisdom. For children this is like a big friend promising to tell the full story so no questions are left. For example a child who listens to the complete tale understands everything clearly. Detailed meaning: Krishna assures that this knowledge encompasses everything leaving no gap. This is the beauty of transcendental wisdom where all questions are answered. The verse makes you curious to receive this full revelation. Practice: open your heart to receive both worldly and spiritual knowledge so your understanding becomes whole and nothing remains unknown.

shlokas.sanskrit:
मनुष्याणां सहस्रेषु कश्चिद्यतति सिद्धये |
यततामपि सिद्धानां कश्चिन्मां वेत्ति तत्त्वतः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
manuṣyāṇāṁ sahasreṣu kaścid yatati siddhaye
yatatām api siddhānāṁ kaścin māṁ vetti tattvataḥ
shlokas.translation: Out of many thousands among men one may endeavor for perfection and of those who have achieved perfection hardly one knows Me in truth.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna notes that among thousands of people only a few strive for perfection and among those who succeed even fewer truly know the divine. This verse highlights the rarity of complete realization. For people in their thirties pursuing excellence this verse brings perspective. For example a leader who works hard for success realizes that true spiritual knowing is rare and requires special grace. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows the rarity of genuine knowledge of the Supreme even among the perfected. This underscores the value of the path of knowledge and wisdom. The verse makes readers eager to be among the few. Practice: strive sincerely for perfection while seeking the grace to know the divine in truth so you become one of the rare realized souls.
shlokas.genz: Krishna notes that among thousands of people only a few strive for perfection and among those who succeed even fewer truly know the divine. This verse highlights the rarity of complete realization. For young adults chasing goals this verse brings perspective. For example a creator who grinds for success realizes that true spiritual knowing is rare and requires special grace. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows the rarity of genuine knowledge of the Supreme even among the perfected. This underscores the value of the path of knowledge and wisdom. The verse makes you eager to be among the few. Practice: strive sincerely for perfection while seeking the grace to know the divine in truth so you become one of the rare realized souls.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna notes that among thousands of people only a few strive for perfection and among those who succeed even fewer truly know the divine. This verse highlights the rarity of complete realization. For children this is like knowing only a few friends try hard to learn the big secret and even fewer really understand it. For example a child who works hard to learn a story realizes only special ones know the full meaning. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows the rarity of genuine knowledge of the Supreme even among the perfected. This underscores the value of the path of knowledge and wisdom. The verse makes you curious to be among the few. Practice: strive sincerely for perfection while seeking the grace to know the divine in truth so you become one of the rare realized souls.

shlokas.sanskrit:
भूमिरापोऽनलो वायुः खं मनो बुद्धिरेव च |
अहङ्कार इतीयं मे भिन्ना प्रकृतिरष्टधा ||
shlokas.transliteration:
bhūmir āpo ’nalo vāyuḥ khaṁ mano buddhir eva ca
ahaṅkāra itīyaṁ me bhinnā prakṛtir aṣṭadhā
shlokas.translation: Earth, water, fire, air, ether, mind, intelligence and false ego—all together these eight constitute My separated material energies.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna lists the eight material elements earth water fire air ether mind intelligence and false ego as His separated energies. This verse begins the description of material nature. For people in their thirties who analyze systems this verse provides the framework. For example a scientist who studies these elements sees them as manifestations of the divine energy. Detailed meaning: Krishna reveals the composition of the material world as His lower nature. This knowledge helps one understand the distinction between matter and spirit. The verse makes readers eager to learn more about the higher nature. Practice: observe the material elements around you and remember they are divine energies so your perspective shifts toward the spiritual.
shlokas.genz: Krishna lists the eight material elements earth water fire air ether mind intelligence and false ego as His separated energies. This verse begins the description of material nature. For young adults who explore science and self this verse provides the framework. For example a thinker who studies these elements sees them as manifestations of the divine energy. Detailed meaning: Krishna reveals the composition of the material world as His lower nature. This knowledge helps one understand the distinction between matter and spirit. The verse makes you eager to learn more about the higher nature. Practice: observe the material elements around you and remember they are divine energies so your perspective shifts toward the spiritual.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna lists the eight material elements earth water fire air ether mind intelligence and false ego as His separated energies. This verse begins the description of material nature. For children this is like knowing the world is made of these special parts from the big friend. For example a child who sees soil water fire wind sky thoughts smartness and proud feeling as gifts from the big friend feels wonder. Detailed meaning: Krishna reveals the composition of the material world as His lower nature. This knowledge helps one understand the distinction between matter and spirit. The verse makes you curious to learn more about the higher nature. Practice: observe the material elements around you and remember they are divine energies so your perspective shifts toward the spiritual.

shlokas.sanskrit:
अपरेयमितस्त्वन्यां प्रकृतिं विद्धि मे पराम् |
जीवभूतां महाबाहो ययेदं धार्यते जगत् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
apareyam itas tv anyāṁ prakṛtiṁ viddhi me parām
jīva-bhūtāṁ mahā-bāho yayedaṁ dhāryate jagat
shlokas.translation: Besides these O mighty-armed Arjuna there is another superior energy of Mine which comprises the living entities who are exploiting the resources of this material inferior nature.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna reveals that besides the eight material elements there is a superior energy consisting of the living souls who use the material world. This verse introduces the higher nature. For people in their thirties who study consciousness this verse brings the missing piece. For example a psychologist who realizes souls are the higher energy understands human motivation beyond physical causes. Detailed meaning: Krishna distinguishes the lower material nature from the higher spiritual nature of the souls. This knowledge is essential for wisdom. The verse makes readers eager to explore the higher energy. Practice: recognize the living souls as the superior energy of the divine so you see the world with spiritual eyes.
shlokas.genz: Krishna reveals that besides the eight material elements there is a superior energy consisting of the living souls who use the material world. This verse introduces the higher nature. For young adults who explore consciousness this verse brings the missing piece. For example a thinker who realizes souls are the higher energy understands human motivation beyond physical causes. Detailed meaning: Krishna distinguishes the lower material nature from the higher spiritual nature of the souls. This knowledge is essential for wisdom. The verse makes you eager to explore the higher energy. Practice: recognize the living souls as the superior energy of the divine so you see the world with spiritual eyes.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna reveals that besides the eight material elements there is a superior energy consisting of the living souls who use the material world. This verse introduces the higher nature. For children this is like knowing besides toys and games there are living friends who play with them. For example a child who sees friends as the special energy feels the big world is alive. Detailed meaning: Krishna distinguishes the lower material nature from the higher spiritual nature of the souls. This knowledge is essential for wisdom. The verse makes you curious to explore the higher energy. Practice: recognize the living friends as the superior energy of the divine so you see the world with spiritual eyes.

shlokas.sanskrit:
एतद्योनीनि भूतानि सर्वाणीत्युपधारय |
अहं कृत्स्नस्य जगतः प्रभवः प्रलयस्तथा ||
shlokas.transliteration:
etad-yonīni bhūtāni sarvāṇīty upadhāraya
ahaṁ kṛtsnasya jagataḥ prabhavaḥ pralayas tathā
shlokas.translation: All created beings have their source in these two natures. Of all that is material and all that is spiritual in this world know for certain that I am both the origin and the dissolution.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna states that all beings arise from the two natures material and spiritual and He is the source and dissolution of the entire universe. This verse reveals the divine as the cause of everything. For people in their thirties who analyze creation this verse provides the ultimate answer. For example a researcher who sees the divine as the origin of all phenomena finds deeper meaning in scientific discovery. Detailed meaning: Krishna explains that both material and spiritual worlds originate from and dissolve into Him. This knowledge establishes the Supreme as the foundation of all existence. The verse makes readers eager to know the divine as the cause. Practice: reflect on the divine as the source of everything you see so your understanding of life becomes complete.
shlokas.genz: Krishna states that all beings arise from the two natures material and spiritual and He is the source and dissolution of the entire universe. This verse reveals the divine as the cause of everything. For young adults who question existence this verse provides the ultimate answer. For example a thinker who sees the divine as the origin of all phenomena finds deeper meaning in life. Detailed meaning: Krishna explains that both material and spiritual worlds originate from and dissolve into Him. This knowledge establishes the Supreme as the foundation of all existence. The verse makes you eager to know the divine as the cause. Practice: reflect on the divine as the source of everything you see so your understanding of life becomes complete.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna states that all beings arise from the two natures material and spiritual and He is the source and dissolution of the entire universe. This verse reveals the divine as the cause of everything. For children this is like knowing all toys and friends come from and return to the big friend. For example a child who sees the big friend as the maker of all play feels safe. Detailed meaning: Krishna explains that both material and spiritual worlds originate from and dissolve into Him. This knowledge establishes the Supreme as the foundation of all existence. The verse makes you curious to know the divine as the cause. Practice: reflect on the divine as the source of everything you see so your understanding of life becomes complete.

shlokas.sanskrit:
मत्तः परतरं नान्यत्किञ्चिदस्ति धनञ्जय |
मयि सर्वमिदं प्रोतं सूत्रे मणिगणा इव ||
shlokas.transliteration:
mattaḥ parataraṁ nānyat kiñcid asti dhanañjaya
mayi sarvam idaṁ protaṁ sūtre maṇi-gaṇā iva
shlokas.translation: O conqueror of wealth there is no truth superior to Me. Everything rests upon Me as pearls are strung on a thread.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna declares that nothing is superior to Him and everything rests upon Him like pearls on a thread. This verse establishes the supremacy of the divine. For people in their thirties seeking the ultimate truth this verse provides the foundation. For example a leader who sees all achievements as resting on the divine gains humility and strength. Detailed meaning: Krishna reveals Himself as the support of all existence with nothing higher. This knowledge is the core of wisdom. The verse makes readers eager to rest everything on the divine. Practice: see all aspects of life as resting on the divine like pearls on a thread so your foundation becomes unshakable.
shlokas.genz: Krishna declares that nothing is superior to Him and everything rests upon Him like pearls on a thread. This verse establishes the supremacy of the divine. For young adults seeking the ultimate truth this verse provides the foundation. For example a creator who sees all ideas as resting on the divine gains humility and strength. Detailed meaning: Krishna reveals Himself as the support of all existence with nothing higher. This knowledge is the core of wisdom. The verse makes you eager to rest everything on the divine. Practice: see all aspects of life as resting on the divine like pearls on a thread so your foundation becomes unshakable.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna declares that nothing is superior to Him and everything rests upon Him like pearls on a thread. This verse establishes the supremacy of the divine. For children this is like knowing all toys and stories rest on the big friend like beads on a string. For example a child who sees all play as held by the big friend feels safe. Detailed meaning: Krishna reveals Himself as the support of all existence with nothing higher. This knowledge is the core of wisdom. The verse makes you curious to rest everything on the divine. Practice: see all aspects of life as resting on the divine like pearls on a thread so your foundation becomes unshakable.

shlokas.sanskrit:
रसोऽहमप्सु कौन्तेय प्रभास्मि शशिसूर्ययोः |
प्रणवः सर्ववेदेषु शब्दः खे पौरुषं नृषु ||
shlokas.transliteration:
raso ’ham apsu kaunteya prabhāsmi śaśi-sūryayoḥ
praṇavaḥ sarva-vedeṣu śabdaḥ khe pauruṣaṁ nṛṣu
shlokas.translation: O son of Kuntī I am the taste of water the light of the sun and the moon the syllable om in the Vedic mantras I am the sound in ether and ability in man.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna reveals Himself as the taste in water the light in sun and moon the syllable om in scriptures the sound in space and ability in humans. This verse shows the divine in everyday elements. For people in their thirties who appreciate nature this verse brings sacredness to ordinary things. For example someone who drinks water and remembers the divine taste finds every meal a spiritual experience. Detailed meaning: Krishna manifests in the essential qualities of creation making the world a reminder of the divine. This knowledge turns daily life into worship. The verse makes readers eager to see the divine everywhere. Practice: recognize the divine essence in simple things like water light and sound so your life becomes filled with awareness.
shlokas.genz: Krishna reveals Himself as the taste in water the light in sun and moon the syllable om in scriptures the sound in space and ability in humans. This verse shows the divine in everyday elements. For young adults who appreciate vibes this verse brings sacredness to ordinary things. For example someone who listens to music and remembers the divine sound finds every playlist a spiritual experience. Detailed meaning: Krishna manifests in the essential qualities of creation making the world a reminder of the divine. This knowledge turns daily life into worship. The verse makes you eager to see the divine everywhere. Practice: recognize the divine essence in simple things like water light and sound so your life becomes filled with awareness.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna reveals Himself as the taste in water the light in sun and moon the syllable om in scriptures the sound in space and ability in humans. This verse shows the divine in everyday elements. For children this is like knowing the sweet taste in juice the bright light in the sky the special sound in songs and the strong power in friends. For example a child who drinks water and remembers the big friend feels joy. Detailed meaning: Krishna manifests in the essential qualities of creation making the world a reminder of the divine. This knowledge turns daily life into worship. The verse makes you curious to see the divine everywhere. Practice: recognize the divine essence in simple things like water light and sound so your life becomes filled with awareness.

shlokas.sanskrit:
पुण्यो गन्धः पृथिव्यां च तेजश्चास्मि विभावसौ |
जीवनं सर्वभूतेषु तपश्चास्मि तपस्विषु ||
shlokas.transliteration:
puṇyo gandhaḥ pṛthivyāṁ ca tejaś cāsmi vibhāvasau
jīvanaṁ sarva-bhūteṣu tapaś cāsmi tapasviṣu
shlokas.translation: I am the pure fragrance in the earth the heat in fire the life in all living beings and the penance of all ascetics.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna reveals Himself as the pure scent in earth the heat in fire the life force in all beings and the austerity in ascetics. This verse continues the manifestation in nature. For people in their thirties who appreciate the environment this verse brings reverence. For example someone who smells flowers or feels warmth remembers the divine presence in every sensation. Detailed meaning: Krishna manifests in the vital qualities that sustain creation making the world a constant reminder of the divine. This knowledge deepens appreciation. The verse makes readers eager to perceive the divine in daily experiences. Practice: notice the divine in scents heat life and discipline so your awareness grows constantly.
shlokas.genz: Krishna reveals Himself as the pure scent in earth the heat in fire the life force in all beings and the austerity in ascetics. This verse continues the manifestation in nature. For young adults who appreciate the environment this verse brings reverence. For example someone who smells rain or feels sunlight remembers the divine presence in every sensation. Detailed meaning: Krishna manifests in the vital qualities that sustain creation making the world a constant reminder of the divine. This knowledge deepens appreciation. The verse makes you eager to perceive the divine in daily experiences. Practice: notice the divine in scents heat life and discipline so your awareness grows constantly.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna reveals Himself as the pure scent in earth the heat in fire the life force in all beings and the austerity in ascetics. This verse continues the manifestation in nature. For children this is like knowing the nice smell in soil the warm light in fire the breath in friends and the hard work in stories. For example a child who smells a flower remembers the big friend. Detailed meaning: Krishna manifests in the vital qualities that sustain creation making the world a constant reminder of the divine. This knowledge deepens appreciation. The verse makes you curious to perceive the divine in daily experiences. Practice: notice the divine in scents heat life and discipline so your awareness grows constantly.

shlokas.sanskrit:
बीजं मां सर्वभूतानां विद्धि पार्थ सनातनम् |
बुद्धिर्बुद्धिमतामस्मि तेजस्तेजस्विनामहम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
bījaṁ māṁ sarva-bhūtānāṁ viddhi pārtha sanātanam
buddhir buddhimatām asmi tejas tejasvinām aham
shlokas.translation: O son of Pṛthā know that I am the original seed of all existences the intelligence of the intelligent and the prowess of all powerful men.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna declares Himself as the eternal seed of all beings the intelligence in the intelligent and the strength in the strong. This verse shows the divine as the source of all qualities. For people in their thirties who value intelligence and strength this verse reveals the origin. For example a strategist who attributes his ideas and power to the divine gains humility and greater effectiveness. Detailed meaning: Krishna positions Himself as the root cause of all life intelligence and power. This knowledge completes the understanding of the divine presence. The verse makes readers eager to see the source in their abilities. Practice: recognize the divine as the seed of your intelligence and strength so you use them with gratitude and wisdom.
shlokas.genz: Krishna declares Himself as the eternal seed of all beings the intelligence in the intelligent and the strength in the strong. This verse shows the divine as the source of all qualities. For young adults who value ideas and power this verse reveals the origin. For example a creator who attributes his ideas and energy to the divine gains humility and greater creativity. Detailed meaning: Krishna positions Himself as the root cause of all life intelligence and power. This knowledge completes the understanding of the divine presence. The verse makes you eager to see the source in your abilities. Practice: recognize the divine as the seed of your intelligence and strength so you use them with gratitude and wisdom.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna declares Himself as the eternal seed of all beings the intelligence in the intelligent and the strength in the strong. This verse shows the divine as the source of all qualities. For children this is like knowing the big friend is the seed of all life smart thoughts and strong play. For example a child who feels the big friend in his ideas and power plays with joy. Detailed meaning: Krishna positions Himself as the root cause of all life intelligence and power. This knowledge completes the understanding of the divine presence. The verse makes you curious to see the source in your abilities. Practice: recognize the divine as the seed of your intelligence and strength so you use them with gratitude and wisdom.

shlokas.sanskrit:
बलं बलवतां चाहं कामरागविवर्जितम् |
धर्माविरुद्धो भूतेषु कामोऽस्मि भरतर्षभ ||
shlokas.transliteration:
balaṁ balavatāṁ cāhaṁ kāma-rāga-vivarjitam
dharmāviruddho bhūteṣu kāmo ’smi bharatarṣabha
shlokas.translation: I am the strength of the strong devoid of passion and desire. I am the lust in beings that is not contrary to dharma O best of the Bharatas.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna reveals that He is the strength in the strong when it is free from lust and desire. He is also the desire in living beings that aligns with righteousness. This verse shows how the divine manifests in human qualities within the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties who lead teams or handle responsibilities this verse brings clarity on using power ethically. For example a manager who channels his drive for results without personal greed or unethical shortcuts finds his leadership becomes a channel for higher wisdom and inspires his team toward balanced success. Detailed meaning: Krishna explains that true strength and desire become divine when purified by dharma. This teaching helps one recognize the Supreme in everyday abilities and motivates aligning personal energy with righteousness. The verse makes readers eager to see the divine in their own strengths. Practice: use your abilities and desires only in ways that support righteousness so they become expressions of divine wisdom in your daily work.
shlokas.genz: Krishna reveals that He is the strength in the strong when it is free from lust and desire. He is also the desire in living beings that aligns with righteousness. This verse shows how the divine manifests in human qualities within the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults who build careers or create content this verse brings clarity on using energy ethically. For example a creator who channels his passion for growth without chasing fame or shortcuts finds his work becomes a channel for higher wisdom and connects authentically with his audience. Detailed meaning: Krishna explains that true strength and desire become divine when purified by dharma. This teaching helps one recognize the Supreme in everyday abilities and motivates aligning personal energy with righteousness. The verse makes you eager to see the divine in your own strengths. Practice: use your abilities and desires only in ways that support righteousness so they become expressions of divine wisdom in your daily moves.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna reveals that He is the strength in the strong when it is free from lust and desire. He is also the desire in living beings that aligns with righteousness. This verse shows how the divine manifests in human qualities within the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like knowing the big friend is the power in strong friends when they play fairly without wanting to grab. For example a child who uses his energy to help others without selfishness feels the big friend inside. Detailed meaning: Krishna explains that true strength and desire become divine when purified by righteousness. This teaching helps one recognize the Supreme in everyday abilities and motivates aligning personal energy with righteousness. The verse makes you curious to see the divine in your own strengths. Practice: use your abilities and desires only in ways that support righteousness so they become expressions of divine wisdom in your daily play.

shlokas.sanskrit:
ये चैव सात्त्विका भावा राजसास्तामसाश्च ये |
मत्त एवेति तान्विद्धि न त्वहं तेषु ते मयि ||
shlokas.transliteration:
ye caiva sāttvikā bhāvā rājasās tāmasāś ca ye
matta eveti tān viddhi na tv ahaṁ teṣu te mayi
shlokas.translation: All states of being be they in goodness passion or ignorance are manifested by My energy. I am not in them but they are in Me.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains that all modes of nature goodness passion and ignorance arise from His energy. He is not dependent on them yet they depend on Him. This verse reveals the relationship between the divine and the material modes in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties navigating changing circumstances this verse brings perspective. For example a leader who faces team dynamics driven by different energies understands they all stem from the divine source and uses this insight to guide with wisdom rather than reacting. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the three gunas are expressions of His energy while He remains transcendental. This knowledge helps one transcend the modes through wisdom. The verse makes readers eager to rise above the influences. Practice: observe the modes at work in situations and remember they depend on the divine so you stay centered in higher wisdom.
shlokas.genz: Krishna explains that all modes of nature goodness passion and ignorance arise from His energy. He is not dependent on them yet they depend on Him. This verse reveals the relationship between the divine and the material modes in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults dealing with shifting vibes this verse brings perspective. For example a creator who sees trends driven by different energies understands they all stem from the divine source and uses this insight to create with wisdom rather than reacting. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the three gunas are expressions of His energy while He remains transcendental. This knowledge helps one transcend the modes through wisdom. The verse makes you eager to rise above the influences. Practice: observe the modes at work in situations and remember they depend on the divine so you stay centered in higher wisdom.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that all modes of nature goodness passion and ignorance arise from His energy. He is not dependent on them yet they depend on Him. This verse reveals the relationship between the divine and the material modes in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like knowing all colors of play come from the big friend. He is not in them but they are in Him. For example a child who sees happy sad and busy play understands they all come from the big friend. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the three gunas are expressions of His energy while He remains transcendental. This knowledge helps one transcend the modes through wisdom. The verse makes you curious to rise above the influences. Practice: observe the modes at work in situations and remember they depend on the divine so you stay centered in higher wisdom.

shlokas.sanskrit:
त्रिभिर्गुणमयैर्भावैरेभिः सर्वमिदं जगत् |
मोहितं नाभिजानाति मामेभ्यः परमव्ययम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
tribhir guṇa-mayair bhāvair ebhiḥ sarvam idaṁ jagat
mohitaṁ nābhijānāti mām ebhyaḥ param avyayam
shlokas.translation: Deluded by the three modes of material nature the world does not know Me who am above them and inexhaustible.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains that the entire world is deluded by the three modes of nature and therefore does not recognize Him as the supreme inexhaustible reality beyond them. This verse reveals the cause of ignorance in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties who observe societal confusion this verse brings understanding. For example a strategist who sees decisions driven by greed passion or inertia realizes the delusion and chooses actions rooted in higher wisdom. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the gunas create illusion preventing recognition of the divine. This knowledge empowers one to transcend the modes. The verse makes readers eager to overcome delusion. Practice: observe how the modes influence the world and seek the divine beyond them so ignorance dissolves in your life.
shlokas.genz: Krishna explains that the entire world is deluded by the three modes of nature and therefore does not recognize Him as the supreme inexhaustible reality beyond them. This verse reveals the cause of ignorance in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults who see trends and confusion this verse brings understanding. For example a thinker who sees decisions driven by greed passion or inertia realizes the delusion and chooses actions rooted in higher wisdom. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the gunas create illusion preventing recognition of the divine. This knowledge empowers one to transcend the modes. The verse makes you eager to overcome delusion. Practice: observe how the modes influence the world and seek the divine beyond them so ignorance dissolves in your life.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that the entire world is deluded by the three modes of nature and therefore does not recognize Him as the supreme inexhaustible reality beyond them. This verse reveals the cause of ignorance in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like friends being confused by busy happy or sleepy feelings and missing the big friend beyond. For example a child who sees play driven by different moods realizes the confusion. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the gunas create illusion preventing recognition of the divine. This knowledge empowers one to transcend the modes. The verse makes you curious to overcome delusion. Practice: observe how the modes influence the world and seek the divine beyond them so ignorance dissolves in your life.

shlokas.sanskrit:
दैवी ह्येषा गुणमयी मम माया दुरत्यया |
मामेव ये प्रपद्यन्ते मायामेतां तरन्ति ते ||
shlokas.transliteration:
daivī hy eṣā guṇa-mayī mama māyā duratyayā
mām eva ye prapadyante māyām etāṁ taranti te
shlokas.translation: This divine energy of Mine consisting of the three modes is difficult to overcome but those who have surrendered unto Me easily cross beyond it.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna describes His divine illusion made of the three modes as very hard to transcend. Those who surrender to Him cross it easily. This verse offers the way out of delusion in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties trapped in material patterns this verse brings the solution. For example a professional entangled in office politics finds surrender to higher wisdom frees him from the cycle. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the material illusion is powerful yet surrender to the divine is the simple key to transcendence. This beautiful promise shows the accessibility of liberation. The verse makes readers eager to surrender. Practice: offer your challenges and attachments to the divine so the illusion loses its grip and you cross to freedom.
shlokas.genz: Krishna describes His divine illusion made of the three modes as very hard to transcend. Those who surrender to Him cross it easily. This verse offers the way out of delusion in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults caught in material loops this verse brings the solution. For example someone stuck in social media comparison finds surrender to higher wisdom frees him from the cycle. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the material illusion is powerful yet surrender to the divine is the simple key to transcendence. This beautiful promise shows the accessibility of liberation. The verse makes you eager to surrender. Practice: offer your challenges and attachments to the divine so the illusion loses its grip and you cross to freedom.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna describes His divine illusion made of the three modes as very hard to transcend. Those who surrender to Him cross it easily. This verse offers the way out of delusion in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like a tricky game that is hard to win but surrendering to the big friend makes it easy. For example a child stuck in a confusing play finds help from the big friend and wins. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the material illusion is powerful yet surrender to the divine is the simple key to transcendence. This beautiful promise shows the accessibility of liberation. The verse makes you curious to surrender. Practice: offer your challenges and attachments to the divine so the illusion loses its grip and you cross to freedom.

shlokas.sanskrit:
न मां दुष्कृतिनो मूढाः प्रपद्यन्ते नराधमाः |
माययापहृतज्ञाना आसुरं भावमाश्रिताः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
na māṁ duṣkṛtino mūḍhāḥ prapadyante narādhamāḥ
māyayāpahṛta-jñānā āsuraṁ bhāvam āśritāḥ
shlokas.translation: Those miscreants who are grossly foolish who are the lowest among mankind whose knowledge is stolen by illusion and who partake of the atheistic nature do not surrender unto Me.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains that wicked foolish people whose knowledge is covered by illusion and who follow demonic nature do not surrender to Him. This verse contrasts the deluded with the wise in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties observing negative influences this verse brings understanding. For example a leader who sees colleagues driven by selfish illusion understands why they resist higher principles and chooses to stay aligned with wisdom. Detailed meaning: Krishna describes how illusion and demonic qualities prevent surrender. This insight highlights the importance of protecting knowledge. The verse makes readers eager to avoid such delusion. Practice: guard your knowledge against illusion and choose surrender to the divine so you stay on the path of wisdom.
shlokas.genz: Krishna explains that wicked foolish people whose knowledge is covered by illusion and who follow demonic nature do not surrender to Him. This verse contrasts the deluded with the wise in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults seeing negative vibes this verse brings understanding. For example a friend who sees others driven by selfish illusion understands why they resist higher principles and chooses to stay aligned with wisdom. Detailed meaning: Krishna describes how illusion and demonic qualities prevent surrender. This insight highlights the importance of protecting knowledge. The verse makes you eager to avoid such delusion. Practice: guard your knowledge against illusion and choose surrender to the divine so you stay on the path of wisdom.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that wicked foolish people whose knowledge is covered by illusion and who follow demonic nature do not surrender to Him. This verse contrasts the deluded with the wise in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like naughty friends whose happy knowing is hidden and who do not turn to the big friend. For example a child who sees others acting mean understands why they miss the big love. Detailed meaning: Krishna describes how illusion and demonic qualities prevent surrender. This insight highlights the importance of protecting knowledge. The verse makes you curious to avoid such delusion. Practice: guard your knowledge against illusion and choose surrender to the divine so you stay on the path of wisdom.

shlokas.sanskrit:
चतुर्विधा भजन्ते मां जनाः सुकृतिनोऽर्जुन |
आर्तो जिज्ञासुरर्थार्थी ज्ञानी च भरतर्षभ ||
shlokas.transliteration:
catur-vidhā bhajante māṁ janāḥ su-kṛtino ’rjuna
ārto jijñāsur arthārthī jñānī ca bharatarṣabha
shlokas.translation: O best of the Bharatas four kinds of pious people begin to render devotional service unto Me the distressed the desirer of wealth the inquisitive and the wise.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna states that four types of virtuous people approach Him the distressed those seeking wealth the curious and the wise. This verse classifies the seekers in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties at different life stages this verse shows all can begin. For example someone facing crisis or seeking success starts the journey and can grow toward wisdom. Detailed meaning: Krishna welcomes all sincere seekers regardless of motivation. This inclusive teaching opens the door for everyone. The verse makes readers eager to identify their type and progress. Practice: recognize your current motivation for seeking the divine and allow it to evolve toward pure wisdom.
shlokas.genz: Krishna states that four types of virtuous people approach Him the distressed those seeking wealth the curious and the wise. This verse classifies the seekers in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults at different life stages this verse shows all can begin. For example someone facing crisis or seeking success starts the journey and can grow toward wisdom. Detailed meaning: Krishna welcomes all sincere seekers regardless of motivation. This inclusive teaching opens the door for everyone. The verse makes you eager to identify your type and progress. Practice: recognize your current motivation for seeking the divine and allow it to evolve toward pure wisdom.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna states that four types of virtuous people approach Him the distressed those seeking wealth the curious and the wise. This verse classifies the seekers in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like four kinds of friends coming to the big friend the sad ones the ones wanting toys the curious ones and the smart ones. Detailed meaning: Krishna welcomes all sincere seekers regardless of motivation. This inclusive teaching opens the door for everyone. The verse makes you curious to identify your type and progress. Practice: recognize your current motivation for seeking the divine and allow it to evolve toward pure wisdom.

shlokas.sanskrit:
तेषां ज्ञानी नित्ययुक्त एकभक्तिविशिष्यते |
प्रियो हि ज्ञानिनोऽत्यर्थमहं स च मम प्रियः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
teṣāṁ jñānī nitya-yukta eka-bhaktir viśiṣyate
priyo hi jñānino ’tyartham ahaṁ sa ca mama priyaḥ
shlokas.translation: Of these the wise one who is in full knowledge and is always engaged in pure devotional service is the best. For I am very dear to him and he is dear to Me.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna says among the four types the wise one who is constantly united and has single minded devotion is the best. He is most dear to the divine and the divine is dear to him. This verse highlights the superiority of pure devotion. For people in their thirties deepening relationships this verse shows the highest bond. For example a devotee who maintains steady love for the divine finds reciprocal closeness. Detailed meaning: Krishna elevates the jnani with exclusive devotion as the dearest. This intimate exchange is the pinnacle of knowledge and wisdom. The verse makes readers eager to cultivate single pointed love. Practice: develop exclusive devotion to the divine so the relationship becomes the closest and most loving.
shlokas.genz: Krishna says among the four types the wise one who is constantly united and has single minded devotion is the best. He is most dear to the divine and the divine is dear to him. This verse highlights the superiority of pure devotion. For young adults building connections this verse shows the highest bond. For example a seeker who maintains steady love for the divine finds reciprocal closeness. Detailed meaning: Krishna elevates the jnani with exclusive devotion as the dearest. This intimate exchange is the pinnacle of knowledge and wisdom. The verse makes you eager to cultivate single pointed love. Practice: develop exclusive devotion to the divine so the relationship becomes the closest and most loving.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna says among the four types the wise one who is constantly united and has single minded devotion is the best. He is most dear to the divine and the divine is dear to him. This verse highlights the superiority of pure devotion. For children this is like the friend who loves the big friend with all heart and feels the closest love back. Detailed meaning: Krishna elevates the jnani with exclusive devotion as the dearest. This intimate exchange is the pinnacle of knowledge and wisdom. The verse makes you curious to cultivate single pointed love. Practice: develop exclusive devotion to the divine so the relationship becomes the closest and most loving.

shlokas.sanskrit:
उदाराः सर्व एवैते ज्ञानी त्वात्मैव मे मतम् |
आस्थितः स हि युक्तात्मा मामेवानुत्तमां गतिम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
udārāḥ sarva evaite jñānī tv ātmaiva me matam
āsthitaḥ sa hi yuktātmā mām evānuttamāṁ gatim
shlokas.translation: All these devotees are undoubtedly magnanimous but the wise one who is in knowledge is very dear to Me. Being engaged in My transcendental service he attains the highest goal.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna says all four types of devotees are noble but the wise one with knowledge is especially dear to Him. Such a person attains the supreme destination. This verse affirms the value of knowledge. For people in their thirties pursuing wisdom this verse encourages depth. For example a seeker who combines devotion with understanding reaches the highest state. Detailed meaning: Krishna values all devotees but highlights the jnani as nearest. This knowledge driven devotion leads to the ultimate goal. The verse makes readers eager to deepen their wisdom. Practice: combine your devotion with true knowledge so you become especially dear to the divine and reach the supreme.
shlokas.genz: Krishna says all four types of devotees are noble but the wise one with knowledge is especially dear to Him. Such a person attains the supreme destination. This verse affirms the value of knowledge. For young adults pursuing wisdom this verse encourages depth. For example a seeker who combines devotion with understanding reaches the highest state. Detailed meaning: Krishna values all devotees but highlights the jnani as nearest. This knowledge driven devotion leads to the ultimate goal. The verse makes you eager to deepen your wisdom. Practice: combine your devotion with true knowledge so you become especially dear to the divine and reach the supreme.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna says all four types of devotees are noble but the wise one with knowledge is especially dear to Him. Such a person attains the supreme destination. This verse affirms the value of knowledge. For children this is like all friends are good but the smart one who knows the big friend best is the closest. Detailed meaning: Krishna values all devotees but highlights the jnani as nearest. This knowledge driven devotion leads to the ultimate goal. The verse makes you curious to deepen your wisdom. Practice: combine your devotion with true knowledge so you become especially dear to the divine and reach the supreme.

shlokas.sanskrit:
बहूनां जन्मनामन्ते ज्ञानवान्मां प्रपद्यते |
वासुदेवः सर्वमिति स महात्मा सुदुर्लभः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
bahūnāṁ janmanām ante jñānavān māṁ prapadyate
vāsudevaḥ sarvam iti sa mahātmā sudurlabhaḥ
shlokas.translation: After many births and deaths the wise man surrenders unto Me knowing that Vasudeva is all that is. Such a great soul is very rare.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna says that after many births the wise person surrenders to Him realizing that everything is Vasudeva. Such a great soul is very rare. This verse shows the culmination of wisdom. For people in their thirties reflecting on life journeys this verse brings perspective. For example someone who has experienced many lessons finally sees the divine in all and surrenders completely. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that true wisdom after many lives leads to full surrender and recognition of the divine in everything. This rare realization is the peak of knowledge. The verse makes readers eager to reach this vision. Practice: contemplate the divine as everything so your surrender becomes complete after your experiences.
shlokas.genz: Krishna says that after many births the wise person surrenders to Him realizing that everything is Vasudeva. Such a great soul is very rare. This verse shows the culmination of wisdom. For young adults reflecting on life journeys this verse brings perspective. For example someone who has experienced many lessons finally sees the divine in all and surrenders completely. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that true wisdom after many lives leads to full surrender and recognition of the divine in everything. This rare realization is the peak of knowledge. The verse makes you eager to reach this vision. Practice: contemplate the divine as everything so your surrender becomes complete after your experiences.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna says that after many births the wise person surrenders to Him realizing that everything is Vasudeva. Such a great soul is very rare. This verse shows the culmination of wisdom. For children this is like after many games the smart friend finally sees the big friend in all and hugs him fully. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that true wisdom after many lives leads to full surrender and recognition of the divine in everything. This rare realization is the peak of knowledge. The verse makes you curious to reach this vision. Practice: contemplate the divine as everything so your surrender becomes complete after your experiences.

shlokas.sanskrit:
कामैस्तैस्तैर्हृतज्ञानाः प्रपद्यन्तेऽन्यदेवताः |
तं तं नियममास्थाय प्रकृत्या नियताः स्वया ||
shlokas.transliteration:
kāmais tais tair hṛta-jñānāḥ prapadyante ’nya-devatāḥ
taṁ taṁ niyamam āsthāya prakṛtyā niyatāḥ svayā
shlokas.translation: Those whose intelligence has been stolen by material desires surrender unto demigods and follow the particular rules and regulations of worship according to their own natures.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains that people whose knowledge is stolen by desires worship other deities and follow specific rules according to their nature. This verse contrasts true wisdom with delusion. For people in their thirties tempted by quick gains this verse brings caution. For example someone who turns to superficial solutions for success misses the supreme and stays bound by desires. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows how desires cover wisdom leading to worship of lesser powers. This insight warns against distraction. The verse makes readers eager to protect their knowledge. Practice: guard your wisdom from desires and stay focused on the supreme so you avoid lesser paths.
shlokas.genz: Krishna explains that people whose knowledge is stolen by desires worship other deities and follow specific rules according to their nature. This verse contrasts true wisdom with delusion. For young adults tempted by quick fixes this verse brings caution. For example someone who turns to superficial solutions for success misses the supreme and stays bound by desires. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows how desires cover wisdom leading to worship of lesser powers. This insight warns against distraction. The verse makes you eager to protect your knowledge. Practice: guard your wisdom from desires and stay focused on the supreme so you avoid lesser paths.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that people whose knowledge is stolen by desires worship other deities and follow specific rules according to their nature. This verse contrasts true wisdom with delusion. For children this is like friends whose happy knowing is taken by wants and who turn to small games instead of the big friend. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows how desires cover wisdom leading to worship of lesser powers. This insight warns against distraction. The verse makes you curious to protect your knowledge. Practice: guard your wisdom from desires and stay focused on the supreme so you avoid lesser paths.

shlokas.sanskrit:
यो यो यां यां तनुं भक्तः श्रद्धयार्चितुमिच्छति |
तस्य तस्याचलां श्रद्धां तामेव विदधाम्यहम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
yo yo yāṁ yāṁ tanuṁ bhaktaḥ śraddhayārchitum icchhati
tasya tasyācalāṁ śraddhāṁ tāmeva vidadhāmy aham
shlokas.translation: Whatever form a devotee desires to worship with faith I make his faith steady in that form.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains that when a devotee chooses any form to worship with sincere faith He strengthens that faith in exactly that form. This verse shows how the divine supports every sincere seeker in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties exploring different spiritual practices this verse brings reassurance. For example a professional who begins with a specific form of prayer or meditation finds his dedication naturally deepening and leading to greater clarity in life. Detailed meaning: Krishna reveals that the Supreme meets the devotee wherever he is and nurtures his faith so it becomes firm. This is the compassionate nature of divine wisdom that adapts to each individual. The verse makes readers eager to choose their path with sincerity. Practice: choose a form of worship that resonates with you and offer your faith to it knowing the divine will strengthen it and guide you forward.
shlokas.genz: Krishna explains that when a devotee chooses any form to worship with sincere faith He strengthens that faith in exactly that form. This verse shows how the divine supports every sincere seeker in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults trying different spiritual vibes this verse brings reassurance. For example someone who starts with a specific mantra or practice finds his connection naturally deepening and leading to greater clarity in daily life. Detailed meaning: Krishna reveals that the Supreme meets the devotee wherever he is and nurtures his faith so it becomes firm. This is the compassionate nature of divine wisdom that adapts to each individual. The verse makes you eager to choose your path with sincerity. Practice: choose a form of worship that resonates with you and offer your faith to it knowing the divine will strengthen it and guide you forward.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that when a devotee chooses any form to worship with sincere faith He strengthens that faith in exactly that form. This verse shows how the divine supports every sincere seeker in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like a big friend helping you love your favorite story or game more when you choose it with a happy heart. For example a child who picks a special prayer or game finds it becoming more fun and clear. Detailed meaning: Krishna reveals that the Supreme meets the devotee wherever he is and nurtures his faith so it becomes firm. This is the compassionate nature of divine wisdom that adapts to each individual. The verse makes you curious to choose your path with sincerity. Practice: choose a form of worship that resonates with you and offer your faith to it knowing the divine will strengthen it and guide you forward.

shlokas.sanskrit:
स तया श्रद्धया युक्तस्तस्याराधनमीहते |
लभते च ततः कामान्मयैव विहितान्हि तान् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
sa tayā śraddhayā yuktas tasyārādhanam īhate
labhate ca tataḥ kāmān mayaiva vihitān hi tān
shlokas.translation: Endowed with that faith he worships that form and obtains his desired results from Me alone.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains that the devotee endowed with such strengthened faith worships that form and receives his desires fulfilled from the Supreme alone. This verse shows how the divine fulfills sincere prayers in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties who pray for specific outcomes this verse brings understanding. For example a professional who prays for guidance in a career decision finds the answer coming through unexpected but perfect means. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that all results come from the Supreme even when worshipped through different forms. This knowledge removes confusion and builds trust. The verse makes readers eager to see the divine as the true giver. Practice: worship with faith knowing all results come from the Supreme so your prayers bring fulfillment and wisdom.
shlokas.genz: Krishna explains that the devotee endowed with such strengthened faith worships that form and receives his desires fulfilled from the Supreme alone. This verse shows how the divine fulfills sincere prayers in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults who seek guidance through different practices this verse brings understanding. For example someone who prays for clarity in life finds the answer coming through unexpected but perfect means. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that all results come from the Supreme even when worshipped through different forms. This knowledge removes confusion and builds trust. The verse makes you eager to see the divine as the true giver. Practice: worship with faith knowing all results come from the Supreme so your prayers bring fulfillment and wisdom.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that the devotee endowed with such strengthened faith worships that form and receives his desires fulfilled from the Supreme alone. This verse shows how the divine fulfills sincere prayers in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like asking the big friend for help in a game and getting exactly what you need. For example a child who prays with a happy heart finds his wish coming true in the best way. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that all results come from the Supreme even when worshipped through different forms. This knowledge removes confusion and builds trust. The verse makes you curious to see the divine as the true giver. Practice: worship with faith knowing all results come from the Supreme so your prayers bring fulfillment and wisdom.

shlokas.sanskrit:
अन्तवत्तु फलं तेषां तद्भवत्यल्पमेधसाम् |
देवान्देवयजो यान्ति मद्भक्ता यान्ति मामपि ||
shlokas.transliteration:
antavat tu phalaṁ teṣāṁ tad bhavaty alpa-medhasām
de vān deva-yajo yānti mad-bhaktā yānti mām api
shlokas.translation: The results obtained by those of small intelligence are temporary. Those who worship the demigods go to the demigods while My devotees come to Me.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains that the results of worshipping lesser deities are temporary while those who worship Him reach Him. This verse contrasts the temporary and eternal in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties seeking lasting success this verse brings perspective. For example a professional who focuses on higher principles rather than short term gains finds enduring fulfillment. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that devotion to the Supreme yields permanent results while lesser worship gives fleeting benefits. This knowledge guides one toward the eternal. The verse makes readers eager to choose the higher path. Practice: direct your devotion to the Supreme so your efforts bring lasting peace and wisdom.
shlokas.genz: Krishna explains that the results of worshipping lesser deities are temporary while those who worship Him reach Him. This verse contrasts the temporary and eternal in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults seeking lasting impact this verse brings perspective. For example a creator who focuses on higher principles rather than short term trends finds enduring fulfillment. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that devotion to the Supreme yields permanent results while lesser worship gives fleeting benefits. This knowledge guides one toward the eternal. The verse makes you eager to choose the higher path. Practice: direct your devotion to the Supreme so your efforts bring lasting peace and wisdom.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that the results of worshipping lesser deities are temporary while those who worship Him reach Him. This verse contrasts the temporary and eternal in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like short fun from small games versus forever joy from the big friend. For example a child who chooses the big friend finds lasting happiness. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that devotion to the Supreme yields permanent results while lesser worship gives fleeting benefits. This knowledge guides one toward the eternal. The verse makes you curious to choose the higher path. Practice: direct your devotion to the Supreme so your efforts bring lasting peace and wisdom.

shlokas.sanskrit:
अव्यक्तं व्यक्तिमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धयः |
परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
avyaktaṁ vyaktim āpannaṁ manyante mām abuddhayaḥ
paraṁ bhāvam ajānanto mamāvyayam anuttamam
shlokas.translation: Unintelligent men who do not know Me think that I have assumed this form and personality. Due to their lack of knowledge they do not know My higher nature which is imperishable and supreme.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains that those without wisdom think He has taken a temporary form while they fail to know His eternal supreme nature. This verse warns against limited understanding in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties who study different philosophies this verse brings depth. For example a seeker who moves beyond surface forms to the eternal reality finds true wisdom. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the unwise see only the manifested form and miss the imperishable essence. This knowledge reveals the higher truth. The verse makes readers eager to know the eternal nature. Practice: look beyond temporary forms to the eternal divine so your wisdom grows complete.
shlokas.genz: Krishna explains that those without wisdom think He has taken a temporary form while they fail to know His eternal supreme nature. This verse warns against limited understanding in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults exploring different beliefs this verse brings depth. For example a seeker who moves beyond surface forms to the eternal reality finds true wisdom. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the unwise see only the manifested form and miss the imperishable essence. This knowledge reveals the higher truth. The verse makes you eager to know the eternal nature. Practice: look beyond temporary forms to the eternal divine so your wisdom grows complete.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that those without wisdom think He has taken a temporary form while they fail to know His eternal supreme nature. This verse warns against limited understanding in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like seeing only the toy and missing the big friend who made it. For example a child who looks deeper finds the real joy. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the unwise see only the manifested form and miss the imperishable essence. This knowledge reveals the higher truth. The verse makes you curious to know the eternal nature. Practice: look beyond temporary forms to the eternal divine so your wisdom grows complete.

shlokas.sanskrit:
नाहं प्रकाशः सर्वस्य योगमायासमावृतः |
मूढोऽयं नाभिजानाति लोको मामजमव्ययम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
nāhaṁ prakāśaḥ sarvasya yoga-māyā-samāvṛtaḥ
mūḍho ’yaṁ nābhijānāti loko mām ajam avyayam
shlokas.translation: I am never manifest to the foolish and unintelligent. For them I am covered by My eternal creative potency and so the deluded world does not know Me who am unborn and inexhaustible.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains that He remains hidden from the foolish and deluded because His divine energy covers Him. The world does not know His birthless and eternal nature. This verse explains why many miss the divine in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties who see widespread confusion this verse brings understanding. For example a thinker who pierces the veil through sincere inquiry discovers the eternal reality. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that illusion hides the Supreme from the unprepared. This knowledge encourages sincere seeking. The verse makes readers eager to remove the covering. Practice: seek the divine with sincerity so the illusion lifts and you know the birthless eternal truth.
shlokas.genz: Krishna explains that He remains hidden from the foolish and deluded because His divine energy covers Him. The world does not know His birthless and eternal nature. This verse explains why many miss the divine in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults who see widespread confusion this verse brings understanding. For example a thinker who pierces the veil through sincere inquiry discovers the eternal reality. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that illusion hides the Supreme from the unprepared. This knowledge encourages sincere seeking. The verse makes you eager to remove the covering. Practice: seek the divine with sincerity so the illusion lifts and you know the birthless eternal truth.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that He remains hidden from the foolish and deluded because His divine energy covers Him. The world does not know His birthless and eternal nature. This verse explains why many miss the divine in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like a big friend hiding behind a curtain until you look with love. For example a child who searches with a happy heart finds the big friend. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that illusion hides the Supreme from the unprepared. This knowledge encourages sincere seeking. The verse makes you curious to remove the covering. Practice: seek the divine with sincerity so the illusion lifts and you know the birthless eternal truth.

shlokas.sanskrit:
वेदाहं समतीतानि वर्तमानानि चाजुन |
भविष्याणि च भूतानि मां तु वेद न कश्चन ||
shlokas.transliteration:
vedāhaṁ samatītāni vartamānāni cārjuna
bhaviṣyāṇi ca bhūtāni māṁ tu veda na kaścana
shlokas.translation: O Arjuna as the Supreme Personality of Godhead I know everything that has happened in the past all that is happening in the present and all things that are yet to come. I also know all living entities but no one knows Me.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna states that He knows all past present and future and all beings while no one knows Him fully. This verse reveals the divine omniscience in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties seeking guidance this verse brings trust. For example a leader who trusts the divine knowledge of all times makes decisions with confidence. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows His complete knowledge of time and beings while remaining beyond human comprehension. This knowledge inspires surrender. The verse makes readers eager to know Him. Practice: trust the divine knowledge of all times and surrender so you come closer to the Supreme.
shlokas.genz: Krishna states that He knows all past present and future and all beings while no one knows Him fully. This verse reveals the divine omniscience in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults seeking guidance this verse brings trust. For example a thinker who trusts the divine knowledge of all times makes decisions with confidence. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows His complete knowledge of time and beings while remaining beyond human comprehension. This knowledge inspires surrender. The verse makes you eager to know Him. Practice: trust the divine knowledge of all times and surrender so you come closer to the Supreme.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna states that He knows all past present and future and all beings while no one knows Him fully. This verse reveals the divine omniscience in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like the big friend knowing all stories old new and yet to come. For example a child who trusts the big friend feels safe. Detailed meaning: Krishna shows His complete knowledge of time and beings while remaining beyond human comprehension. This knowledge inspires surrender. The verse makes you curious to know Him. Practice: trust the divine knowledge of all times and surrender so you come closer to the Supreme.

shlokas.sanskrit:
इच्छाद्वेषसमुत्थेन द्वन्द्वमोहेन भारत |
सर्वभूतानि सम्मोहं सर्गे यान्ति परन्तप ||
shlokas.transliteration:
icchā-dveṣa-samutthena dvandva-mohena bhārata
sarva-bhūtāni sammohaṁ sarge yānti parantapa
shlokas.translation: O scion of Bharata all living beings are born into delusion bewildered by the dualities of desire and hate O conqueror of the foe.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains that all beings are born into delusion caused by the dualities of desire and hate. This verse shows the root of ignorance in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties dealing with likes and dislikes this verse brings insight. For example a person who recognizes desire and hate as the cause of confusion can begin to transcend them through wisdom. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the pairs of opposites create illusion from birth. This knowledge is the first step to freedom. The verse makes readers eager to overcome duality. Practice: observe desire and hate in your life and seek wisdom to rise above them so delusion fades.
shlokas.genz: Krishna explains that all beings are born into delusion caused by the dualities of desire and hate. This verse shows the root of ignorance in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults dealing with likes and dislikes this verse brings insight. For example a person who recognizes desire and hate as the cause of confusion can begin to transcend them through wisdom. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the pairs of opposites create illusion from birth. This knowledge is the first step to freedom. The verse makes you eager to overcome duality. Practice: observe desire and hate in your life and seek wisdom to rise above them so delusion fades.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that all beings are born into delusion caused by the dualities of desire and hate. This verse shows the root of ignorance in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like feeling happy or sad and getting confused. For example a child who learns to stay calm beyond happy and sad feels clear. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that the pairs of opposites create illusion from birth. This knowledge is the first step to freedom. The verse makes you curious to overcome duality. Practice: observe desire and hate in your life and seek wisdom to rise above them so delusion fades.

shlokas.sanskrit:
येषां त्वन्तगतं पापं जनानां पुण्यकर्मणाम् |
ते द्वन्द्वमोहनिमुक्ता भजन्ते मां दृढव्रताः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
yeṣāṁ tv anta-gataṁ pāpaṁ janānāṁ puṇya-karmaṇām
te dvandva-moha-nirmuktā bhajante māṁ dṛḍha-vratāḥ
shlokas.translation: But those people whose sins have been destroyed by pious activities who are free from the dualities of delusion and who are devoted to Me with firm resolve worship Me.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains that those whose sins are destroyed by pious deeds and who are free from the delusion of dualities worship Him with firm resolve. This verse shows the qualification for true devotion in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties who have lived through challenges this verse brings hope. For example someone who has overcome past mistakes through good actions finds firm devotion and peace. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that purity from sin and freedom from duality lead to steady devotion. This is the state of advanced wisdom. The verse makes readers eager to purify and devote. Practice: perform pious actions and rise above dualities so your devotion becomes firm and steady.
shlokas.genz: Krishna explains that those whose sins are destroyed by pious deeds and who are free from the delusion of dualities worship Him with firm resolve. This verse shows the qualification for true devotion in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults who have overcome struggles this verse brings hope. For example someone who has overcome past mistakes through good actions finds firm devotion and peace. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that purity from sin and freedom from duality lead to steady devotion. This is the state of advanced wisdom. The verse makes you eager to purify and devote. Practice: perform pious actions and rise above dualities so your devotion becomes firm and steady.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that those whose sins are destroyed by pious deeds and who are free from the delusion of dualities worship Him with firm resolve. This verse shows the qualification for true devotion in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like good actions cleaning the heart and staying beyond happy and sad to love the big friend with a strong heart. For example a child who does kind things and stays calm loves the big friend steadily. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that purity from sin and freedom from duality lead to steady devotion. This is the state of advanced wisdom. The verse makes you curious to purify and devote. Practice: perform pious actions and rise above dualities so your devotion becomes firm and steady.

shlokas.sanskrit:
जरामरणमोक्षाय मामाश्रित्य यतन्ति ये |
ते ब्रह्म तद्विदुः कृत्स्नमध्यात्मं कर्म चाखिलम् ||
shlokas.transliteration:
jarā-maraṇa-mokṣāya mām āśritya yatanti ye
te brahma tad viduḥ kṛtsnam adhyātmaṁ karma cākhilam
shlokas.translation: Those who take refuge in Me and strive for liberation from old age and death know the entire Brahman the individual self and all the activities of the material world.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna explains that those who take refuge in Him and strive for freedom from old age and death know Brahman the self and all actions completely. This verse shows the reward of surrender in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For people in their thirties seeking liberation from cycles this verse gives the goal. For example someone who surrenders and strives understands the whole reality. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that surrender and effort for liberation bring complete knowledge. This is the culmination of wisdom. The verse makes readers eager to strive. Practice: take refuge in the divine and strive for freedom from cycles so you gain complete knowledge.
shlokas.genz: Krishna explains that those who take refuge in Him and strive for freedom from old age and death know Brahman the self and all actions completely. This verse shows the reward of surrender in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For young adults seeking liberation from cycles this verse gives the goal. For example someone who surrenders and strives understands the whole reality. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that surrender and effort for liberation bring complete knowledge. This is the culmination of wisdom. The verse makes you eager to strive. Practice: take refuge in the divine and strive for freedom from cycles so you gain complete knowledge.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna explains that those who take refuge in Him and strive for freedom from old age and death know Brahman the self and all actions completely. This verse shows the reward of surrender in the path of knowledge and wisdom. For children this is like taking help from the big friend and trying hard to be free from sad things and knowing everything. For example a child who asks the big friend and tries hard understands all games. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that surrender and effort for liberation bring complete knowledge. This is the culmination of wisdom. The verse makes you curious to strive. Practice: take refuge in the divine and strive for freedom from cycles so you gain complete knowledge.

shlokas.sanskrit:
साधिभूताधिदैवं मां साधियज्ञं च ये विदुः |
प्रयाणकालेऽपि च मां ते विदुर्युक्तचेतसः ||
shlokas.transliteration:
sādhibhūtādhidaivaṁ māṁ sādhiyajñaṁ ca ye viduḥ
prayāṇa-kāle ’pi ca māṁ te vidur yukta-cetasaḥ
shlokas.translation: Those who know Me as the Supreme Lord the governing principle of the material manifestation and the demigods and as the one who is to be known as the Supreme Personality of Godhead and the one who is to be worshiped by the performance of sacrifice can understand Me even at the time of death.
shlokas.millennial: Krishna says those who know Him as the Supreme in the material world the demigods and the object of sacrifice know Him even at the time of death. This verse shows the power of complete knowledge. For people in their thirties preparing for life's end this verse brings peace. For example someone who knows the divine in all aspects faces death with clarity. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that full knowledge of the Supreme ensures remembrance at the final moment. This is the perfection of knowledge and wisdom. The verse makes readers eager to know Him fully. Practice: know the divine in all aspects of life so you remember Him at every moment including the last.
shlokas.genz: Krishna says those who know Him as the Supreme in the material world the demigods and the object of sacrifice know Him even at the time of death. This verse shows the power of complete knowledge. For young adults facing uncertainty this verse brings peace. For example someone who knows the divine in all aspects faces challenges with clarity. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that full knowledge of the Supreme ensures remembrance at the final moment. This is the perfection of knowledge and wisdom. The verse makes you eager to know Him fully. Practice: know the divine in all aspects of life so you remember Him at every moment including the last.
shlokas.genalpha: Krishna says those who know Him as the Supreme in the material world the demigods and the object of sacrifice know Him even at the time of death. This verse shows the power of complete knowledge. For children this is like knowing the big friend in all games and stories and remembering him always. For example a child who knows the big friend everywhere feels safe at bedtime. Detailed meaning: Krishna teaches that full knowledge of the Supreme ensures remembrance at the final moment. This is the perfection of knowledge and wisdom. The verse makes you curious to know Him fully. Practice: know the divine in all aspects of life so you remember Him at every moment including the last.
